etymology.description: semantic 足 (zú, 'foot') + phonetic 各 (gè) -> road
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 足 (zú, 'foot') represents walking or movement, suggesting the idea of a path or road. 各 (gè) serves as the phonetic component, contributing to the pronunciation of the character.
etymology.simplification: The character retains its form in simplified Chinese, with no changes.

examples.chinese: 这里的路很宽敞(這裡的路很寬敞)。
examples.english: The road here is very wide.

examples.chinese: 他选择了一条不同的路(他選擇了一條不同的路)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xuǎnzéle yī tiáo bùtóng de lù.

examples.chinese: 走这条路会更快(走這條路會更快)。
examples.pinyin: Zǒu zhè tiáo lù huì gèng kuài.
examples.english: Taking this road will be faster.
